decks.thematic: Shared world layer of neutral encounters, roaming creatures, opportunistic events, and generic pressures that can touch any faction. This is less about tribe or race identity and more about map texture, uncertainty, travel danger, and emergent stories; it adds flexible utility, surprise pivots, and world-state moments that make every run feel less scripted.
decks.fantasy: Neutral Middle-earth world texture: roads, wilderness, chance meetings, danger, and emergent travel stories that sit outside any single faction identity.
mechanicalPillars: neutral encounters that change board texture
avoidThemes: too many cards built around one creature type
generic filler that lacks travel-story flavor

decks.thematic: Common action utility deck shared by all factions: stealth, theft, rumors, and practical tactical tricks that are not faction-locked.
decks.fantasy: Universal low-magic action deck for cunning, opportunism, and practical tricks anyone in Middle-earth might attempt.
avoidThemes: high wizardry as the primary focus
overly ornamental effects with no tactical purpose

decks.thematic: Common spell/event deck shared by all factions for lightweight magical or celebratory effects usable beyond faction identity.
decks.fantasy: Shared light-magic and wonder layer: blessings, festival effects, omens, and broadly usable magical moments not owned by one faction.

decks.thematic: Core Free Peoples deck built around Men, Elves, Dwarves, Hobbits, Eagles, and other allied peoples working in concert. Its gameplay favors balanced development, card quality, scouting, diplomacy, defensive resilience, region access, and reliable utility over brute specialization; it should feel like the broad strategic foundation from which the more sharply themed Gandalf subdecks branch outward.
decks.fantasy: Broad alliance-of-the-Free-Peoples foundation: cooperation, guidance, resilience, and adaptable responses across many races and regions.

decks.thematic: High-authority comeback deck built around renewal, courage, cleansing, and open leadership after the return in white. This deck should reward stabilization, morale swings, anti-fear and anti-corruption play, stronger army support, and decisive rally turns; it suits players who want to absorb pressure, reclaim control, and then swing the board through righteous momentum.
decks.fantasy: Returned white authority: cleansing light, restored morale, command presence, and decisive renewal after despair.
avoidThemes: purely contemplative lore with no board impact
overuse of resurrection imagery on every card

decks.thematic: Pure wizardry deck focused on refined spellcraft, insight, wards, lore, and precise magical intervention. Elven wisdom, hidden knowledge, vision, revelation, and tightly targeted magic all belong here; in gameplay it emphasizes selective control, information advantage, safe setup, and elegant spell resolution rather than broad swarm or raw military output.
decks.fantasy: Gandalf as sage-mage: hidden lore, wards, visions, and exact magical responses grounded in wisdom rather than spectacle.

decks.thematic: Stormcrow is the Gandalf subdeck of uneasy wartime pressure among Men. It should frame the Grey Pilgrim not only as an ill omen or unwelcome counselor, but as the unsettling force who appears when Mannish realms are strained by encroaching evil and must harden themselves for survival. This includes fronts such as Rohan against the White Hand, Gondor against Mordor, the Northmen of Rhovanion against Easterlings, the Anduin Vale against Dol Guldur, Bree and wider Eriador under the long memory of Angmar, and similar regions where roads, ports, fords, courts, and frontier settlements live under gathering shadow. Gandalf is trying to help, organize, and strengthen resistance, yet many still see him as the sign that peace has already failed. In gameplay the deck should lean into morale strain, defensive hardening, political tension, frontier vigilance, war-readiness, movement pressure, intelligence and concealment, loyalty under stress, and reputation-based disruption rather than reducing itself to repeated warnings or bad-news delivery.
decks.fantasy: Gandalf as Stormcrow in the wars of Men: a mistrusted but necessary presence who unsettles courts, hardens defenses, stirs movement, and marks the moment when ordinary peace gives way to border fear, mobilization, and survival under shadow.
mechanicalPillars: reputation-driven disruption among Men and their foes
intelligence, concealment, and timing advantage under gathering shadow
themeLanes: wars of Men against evil powers on threatened frontiers
ports, roads, fords, courts, and border holds under strain
political tension and loyalty under wartime pressure
uneasy mobilization, watchfulness, and defensive preparation
grim aid that strengthens resistance while spreading unease
avoidThemes: reducing the deck to generic weather magic
collapsing every card into warnings, messages, rumors, or tidings
overly clean high-authority leadership detached from wartime pressure
visualDirections: windswept arrivals at halls, gates, fords, ports, and border holds
ominous silhouettes before wary Men under arms
roads, courts, musters, harbors, and frontier war watches under uneasy shadow
mobilization, defensive preparation, and strained human settlements facing the dark

decks.thematic: Mischief, adventure, and social-chaos deck built around hobbits and the wider road-story tradition of Middle-earth: Bilbo's unexpected wanderings, Frodo's early misadventures, Gandalf's intrusive arrivals, and the hobbit-side companionship of the Fellowship as seen in the first book. Its regional flavor should stretch across Eriador and beyond, especially Bree-land, the roads and inns of the North, the Greenway and other long roads, the Old Forest, the Barrow-downs, the Misty Mountains, Rivendell, the Anduin River, Lothlorien-adjacent journeys, the Lonely Mountain road toward Lake-town and the Woodland Realm, and the far reaches of the story such as Saruman's taking of the Shire, hobbits in Isengard, and the lonely trip to Mordor. The deck should hold wandering locals, petty troublemakers, rural rumors, travel incidents, river crossings, mountain passes, elf-halls, eerie borderland hazards, captivity, and small but meaningful frontier entanglements. It is less martial than other branches and more about quirky utility, board disturbance, off-angle events, and playful disruption that feels fair but annoying for the opponent.
decks.fantasy: A wandering road-story deck where Bilbo-style detours, Frodo's long journey, Gandalf's inconvenient meddling, the company of the first book, and the hobbits' far-flung adventures from the Shire to Mordor turn small interruptions into meaningful mischief.
themeLanes: the Greenway and other long roads
avoidThemes: repeating food-party-fireworks jokes as the dominant identity
making every card a hobbit feast variant
art prompts that keep reusing the same table, mug, or party scene
visualDirections: awkward social scenes instead of pure celebration

decks.thematic: Dwarf-friend archetype rooted in mountains, halls, treasure, mithril, endurance, and old alliances with the Durin folk. Expect Dwarven races, Erebor and Khazad themes, defensive economy, sturdy progression, fortress-like value, and reward for treasure or mountain cards; this deck suits slow-building players who want resilience, gear, and hard-to-shift board presence.
decks.fantasy: Gandalf as dwarf-friend: mountain roads, ancient halls, treasure-lore, and hard endurance tied to the houses of Durin.
avoidThemes: thin gimmicks without mountain or dwarf resonance
visualDirections: sturdy shieldwall and crafted relic imagery

decks.thematic: Core Isengard strategy deck blending manipulation, coercion, industrial preparation, mixed evil-men support, and practical force. Its races and allies can include Dunlendings, opportunistic Men, Orcs, and wizard-led control pieces; in play it is about pressure through planning, resource leverage, positioning, political influence, and midrange control before committing to a sharper Saruman identity.
decks.fantasy: The broad Isengard foundation: planning, coercion, furnaces, mixed servants, and practical cruelty in service of controlled expansion.

decks.thematic: Orthanc mastermind deck focused on rhetoric, control, persuasion, voice, and polished strategic dominance. This is Saruman as the calculating high wizard who bends choices and states rather than simply rushing with troops; players should expect influence, deception, mind-affecting actions, elegant tactical denial, and carefully timed control cards.
decks.fantasy: Saruman as silver-tongued master of Orthanc, winning by voice, status, and controlled perception rather than raw rush.
avoidThemes: simple brute-force combat as core identity
visualDirections: tower chambers and high vantage persuasion

decks.thematic: Arcane self-reinvention deck expressing Saruman's many-coloured identity: layered wizardry, vanity, broad magical reach, and power drawn from synthesis rather than purity. This subdeck is ideal for spell-oriented play with multi-axis magical effects, buffs and debuffs in the same action, symbolic robes-and-voice flavor, and flashy board manipulation that feels sophisticated and dangerous.
decks.fantasy: Saruman's fractured many-coloured persona: self-stylized wizardry, synthesis, vanity, illusion, and magical excess presented as sophistication.
avoidThemes: reducing the deck to robe jokes only
plain military cards without arcane vanity

decks.thematic: War engine of Isengard built around Uruk-hai, Orc industry, furnaces, intimidation, and relentless production-to-pressure conversion. This is the aggressive Saruman branch: siege tools, military events, battlefield force multiplication, and sustained assault through disciplined evil infantry and industrial throughput rather than finesse.
decks.fantasy: Isengard's forged war machine: Uruk discipline, furnace output, siege craft, and fear-driven aggression made systematic.
avoidThemes: soft wizard rhetoric as the main emphasis
too many identical infantry charge cards

decks.thematic: Foundational Shadow empire deck covering Mordor's broad military, corruption, fear, conquest, and attrition game plan. Orcs, evil men, monsters, siege assets, and oppressive utility belong here, giving a reliable shell for expansion into more specialized Sauron identities; it rewards steady pressure, resource denial, battlefield cruelty, and inevitability through accumulation.
decks.fantasy: The broad machinery of Mordor: armies, monsters, tyranny, and corruption grinding forward through fear and accumulation.
avoidThemes: too much occult horror at the expense of empire
isolated gimmicks with no oppressive throughline
visual sameness of one fortress angle

decks.thematic: Lidless surveillance and Nazgul command deck centered on dread presence, revelation, control auras, and the feeling that no movement escapes Mordor's notice. It favors fear, despair, board locking, command projection, cursed riders, and oppressive events that squeeze the opponent's options turn by turn until resistance becomes inefficient.
decks.fantasy: The Eye watching through Nazgul terror: surveillance, revelation, dread, and command pressure that makes every movement feel seen.
avoidThemes: repeating a single eye symbol without scene variety

decks.thematic: Subversion-and-false-gifts deck for Sauron as Annatar and corrupter: persuasive command, fallen Numenorean legacy, palantir theft, temptation, and domination achieved through trust, seduction, and crooked counsel before open conquest.
decks.fantasy: Sauron as Annatar and the Deceiver: false gifts, seductive promises, corrupted loyalties, and domination won through trust before open war.
themeLanes: the Mouth of Sauron and crooked diplomacy
avoidThemes: Nazgul surveillance as the dominant identity
monster-horror swamping the social corruption theme
siege-industrial visuals taking over the deck

decks.thematic: Dol Guldur horror deck built around dark sorcery, ruined strongholds, cursed creatures, lingering effects, and monster-heavy occult pressure. Goblins, spirits, spiders, dragons, undead presences, and slow magical decay all fit here; gameplay leans toward attrition, haunting pressure, corruption-adjacent status play, and a more monstrous, supernatural style than the disciplined Dark Eye branch.
decks.fantasy: Sauron as the Necromancer of Dol Guldur: ruin, occult infestation, cursed creatures, and slow supernatural decay spreading from dark strongholds.
avoidThemes: repeating one spider or ghost image endlessly
plain battlefield combat with no horror tone
eye-surveillance themes overtaking the horror identity
